name: Umar ibn Hussain
title: Young Legacy of the Master of Martyrs
shortDescription: One of Imam Hussain's (AS) young sons who was present in Karbala and, despite his young age, was taken captive by the Kufan army.
fullStory:
Umar ibn al-Hussain was one of Imam Hussain's (AS) young sons whose name is mentioned among the captives of the Ashura event. His mother was Rabab (the Imam's loyal wife), making him the brother of Ali Asghar and Sakina.

On the day of Ashura, Umar ibn Hussain, who was a young child, witnessed the painful martyrdom of his infant brother (Ali Asghar) and his noble father. After the event of Ashura, the enemies, disregarding his age, took him captive like the other children.

He traveled the long route to Kufa and Sham, enduring immense suffering and seeing severed heads atop spears.

Some historical sources report that Yazid, in the palace of Sham, upon seeing Umar ibn Hussain, called his own son (Khalid) to humiliate the Ahl al-Bayt and said to Umar: 'Will you wrestle with my son?' Umar, with the bravery inherited from his father, replied: 'Give both of us a knife to fight together so you can see who wins!' Yazid was amazed by this response and said: 'A snake begets nothing but a snake.'
quoteArabic: أَعْطِنِي سِكِّيناً وَأَعْطِهِ سِكِّيناً، ثُمَّ أُقاتِلُهُ...
quote: (In response to Yazid asking him to wrestle his son:) Give both of us a knife to fight so you can see who wins!
